content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable Larry Stirling 
Assemblymember, 77th District 
7777 Alvarado Road, Suite 377 
Le Mesa, CA 
92041 
Dear Mr. Stirling: 
June 3, 1987 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 87-143 
We have received your letter requesting advice concerning the 
duties of Signal Environmental Systems, Inc., under the campaign 
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.lI 
Your letter 
does not indicate that Signal Environmental Systems has authorized 
you to request advice on its behalf. We cannot provide written 
advice to you concerning the duties of a third party unless that 
third party has specifically authorized you to act as its representa-
tive. 
(Section 83114(b»; Regulation 18329(b) (8) (B), copy enclosed.) 
If you are the authorized representative of Signal Environmental 
Systems, we will require a written statement to that effect, the 
mailing address of the corporation, and the name of the person who 
provided authorization. 
(Regulation 18329(b) (2).) 
We also will 
require specific information as to the activities of the corporation 
regarding the proposed local ballot measure. 
If you have any questions concerning this letter, please contact 
me at (916) 322-5901. 
KED:km 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
cf{~t.'b~ 
By: Kathryn E. Donovan 
Counsel, Legal Division 
1I 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code 
section 18000, et seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804,0807 • (916) 322,5660 


May 8, 1987 
John H. Larson 
Chair 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
Dear Mr. Larson: 
Signal Environmental Systems, Inc., a New Hampshire corporation, 
is currently the developer of a proposed waste-to-energy facility 
to be located in the City of San Diego. 
Signal has conducted an extensive public relations effort in many 
communities in San Diego County over the last two years. 
Currently, a group of citizens is in the process of attempting to 
qualify a local measure for a future vote regarding the issue on 
a forthcoming San Diego City election. 
What responsibility does Signal Environmental Systems have under 
the provisions of the Political Reform Act? 
Specifically, I would appreciate an opinion on whether they need 
to file campaign disclosure reports, and if so, when and where 
would those reports be filed? 
Please reply directly to me at my 
district office. 
Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. 
Sincerely, 
7th District 
LS:ws 
SF 6/8 
of 
de 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Honorable Larry stirling 
Assemblyman, 77th District 
state Capitol 
P.o. Box 942849 
Sacramento, CA 
94249-0001 
Dear Assemblyman Stirling: 
May 22, 1987 
Re: 
87-143 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act was received on May 19, 1987 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact Kathryn E. Donovan, an attorney 
in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or more information is needed, you should expect a response 
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written 
advice. 
If your request is for informal assistance, we will 
answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission Regulation 
18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware 
that your letter and our response are public records which may 
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for 
disclosure. 
DMG:plh 
Very truly yours, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(Q I h'l 1.")") .r::.kk{'\

sections.question: What responsibility does Signal Environmental Systems have under the provisions of the Political Reform Act? Specifically, I would appreciate an opinion on whether they need to file campaign disclosure reports, and if so, when and where would those reports be filed?
sections.conclusion: Your letter does not indicate that Signal Environmental Systems has authorized you to request advice on its behalf. We cannot provide written advice to you concerning the duties of a third party unless that third party has specifically authorized you to act as its representative. If you are the authorized representative of Signal Environmental Systems, we will require a written statement to that effect, the mailing address of the corporation, and the name of the person who provided authorization. We also will require specific information as to the activities of the corporation regarding the proposed local ballot measure.
sections.question_synthetic: An Assemblymember requests advice on whether Signal Environmental Systems, Inc., a corporation developing a waste-to-energy facility in San Diego, must file campaign disclosure reports under the Political Reform Act regarding a proposed local ballot measure.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide written advice regarding a third party's campaign disclosure obligations without written authorization from that party confirming the requester's representative status and providing specific information about the corporation's activities.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains three letters: incoming request (May 8), acknowledgment of receipt (May 22), and FPPC response (June 3). The June 3 letter is the primary advice response. No formal CONCLUSION section header, but the substantive response is clearly present in the second substantive paragraph.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What responsibility does Signal Environmental Systems have under the provisions of the Political Reform Act? Specifically, I would appreciate an opinion on whether they need to file campaign disclosure reports, and if so, when and where would those reports be filed?

CONCLUSION: Your letter does not indicate that Signal Environmental Systems has authorized you to request advice on its behalf. We cannot provide written advice to you concerning the duties of a third party unless that third party has specifically authorized you to act as its representative. If you are the authorized representative of Signal Environmental Systems, we will require a written statement to that effect, the mailing address of the corporation, and the name of the person who provided authorization. We also will require specific information as to the activities of the corporation regarding the proposed local ballot measure.

embedding.summary: Assemblymember Larry Stirling requested FPPC advice on whether Signal Environmental Systems, Inc. must file campaign disclosure reports regarding a proposed local ballot measure in San Diego. The FPPC declined to provide the requested advice, stating it requires written authorization from the corporation confirming Stirling's representative status and specific information about the corporation's activities.
